description: pip tar/ZIP concatenated file recognition behavior conflict, may install unexpected content
reason: upstream advisory currently has no fixed version published
mitigation: keep audit failure active for all other CVEs
review_trigger: remove ignore once fixed version appears
resolution_criteria: CVE-2026-3219 fixed version published on PyPI advisory DB

description: product-tests job fails in CI on ubuntu-latest with no annotations, while all 737 tests pass locally in every configuration tested (clean env, PYTHONDEVMODE=1, -W error reveals 16 warnings but no errors).
reason: Likely CI runner environment difference — cannot reproduce locally across 5+ configurations
mitigation: All 737 tests pass locally (uv run pytest tests/unit/product -q)
governance-self-check, verify-native, ruff, governance-tests all pass in CI
This is a CI signal quality issue, not a code defect
Re-run CI job to check if transient
If persistent: add verbose output (remove -q) to CI step for diagnosis
review_trigger: Re-run CI product-tests job or push new commit
resolution_criteria: product-tests passes in CI or root cause identified with evidence

description: Debt JSONL ledger has no entry-level integrity protection. Debt status can be changed from OPEN to CLOSED with no evidence, and ordivon-verify does NOT detect the change. The verification-debt checker validates ledger structure but not individual entry integrity.
reason: JSONL ledgers are plain text — no checksum, audit trail, or signature on individual entries
mitigation: Short-term: add debt-entry integrity check to verification-debt checker
Medium-term: add content-hash to each debt entry
Long-term: consider signed commits or git-based audit trail
review_trigger: Next red-team audit or debt system modification
resolution_criteria: Modifying debt entry status without evidence is detected and BLOCKED by at least one CI checker

description: Checker Python source code can be modified to inject blind spots (e.g., adding entries to GOVERNED_EXCLUSIONS). No CI job verifies checker source integrity or detects modification of governance-critical constants. An unregistered file was successfully hidden by modifying the exclusion list.
reason: Checker source code has no integrity verification — modifications are git-tracked but not automatically detected post-merge
mitigation: Short-term: add checker integrity self-check to governance-self-check CI job
Medium-term: extract all authority sets (exclusions, safe files, etc.) to governed JSON schemas
Long-term: content-addressable checker manifests with hash verification
review_trigger: Next checker source modification or red-team audit
resolution_criteria: Checker source modification that creates a governance blind spot is detected by CI
